abilities.description: Roll the Deed Die (1d3 at this level) alongside your attack die. Declare a stunt before rolling. If the attack hits AND the Deed Die shows 3+, the stunt also succeeds automatically. On a miss, the deed fails but the die can still be used defensively at the judge's discretion.
abilities.name: Mighty Deed of Arms

abilities.description: Reposition yourself or your foe, gain a positional advantage, or set up an ally's next action.
abilities.name: Deed: Trick

abilities.description: Deal extra damage equal to the Deed Die result, driving your foe back.
abilities.name: Deed: Beat Down

abilities.description: Force the target to make a Will save or become demoralized, granting you a bonus to your next attack against it.
abilities.name: Deed: Intimidate

abilities.description: Knock the target prone in addition to dealing damage.
abilities.name: Deed: Knock Down

abilities.description: Damage or destroy the target's weapon, shield, or armor instead of (or in addition to) the target.
abilities.name: Deed: Sunder

abilities.description: Gain a bonus to AC or a save until your next action, protecting yourself or an adjacent ally.
abilities.name: Deed: Guard

abilities.description: Attack roll: 1d20 + 3 (Str + deed die eligible). Damage: 1d8 + 2 (Str), plus Deed Die if a Mighty Deed is declared.
abilities.name: Longsword Attack

abilities.description: Attack roll: 1d20 + 1 (Agi). Damage: 1d6. Mighty Deeds may also be attempted on ranged attacks.
abilities.name: Shortbow Attack

abilities.description: Warriors may spend a point of Luck to reroll any single attack roll, damage roll, or Fort/Ref/Will save. Spent Luck recovers slowly between adventures (judge's discretion), not on a simple rest.
abilities.name: Luck Reroll
